name: extensions
context: SQLite extensions — FTS5 full-text search, JSON functions, R-Tree spatial indexing, math functions, and loadable modules
info: SQLite ships with several powerful built-in extensions and supports loading third-party extensions at runtime. FTS5 provides full-text search with BM25 ranking, snippet extraction, and multiple tokenizers. JSON functions (json_extract, ->, ->>, json_each, json_set) enable first-class JSON column support. R-Tree provides spatial indexing for geographic and range queries. Math functions (sin, cos, log, sqrt) are available since SQLite 3.35.0. Custom extensions can be loaded from shared libraries (.so/.dylib/.dll) to add user-defined functions, virtual tables, collating sequences, and VFS implementations. Agents use this knowledge when they need full-text search, JSON column operations, spatial queries, or custom database functions. See [[sqlite/queries]] for JSON query syntax and [[sqlite/schema]] for creating FTS5 virtual tables.
body: # SQLite Extensions SQLite ships with powerful built-in extensions and supports loading custom extensions at runtime. This reference covers the most important ones. ## FTS5 — Full-Text Search FTS5 is SQLite's full-text search engine. It creates a virtual table with an inverted index, enabling fast text search with ranking. ### Create an FTS5 Table ```sql -- Basic FTS5 table CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE articles_fts USING fts5( title, body, content='articles', -- external content table content_rowid='id' -- map to source table's PK ); -- Standalone FTS5 table (stores its own content) CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE search_index USING fts5(title, body); -- With tokenizer configuration CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE docs USING fts5( title, content, tokenize='porter unicode61 remove_diacritics 1' ); -- With prefix indexes for fast prefix queries CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE docs USING fts5( title, content, prefix='2 3' ); ``` ### Query FTS5 ```sql -- Basic search SELECT * FROM articles_fts WHERE articles_fts MATCH 'database optimization'; -- Search specific column SELECT * FROM articles_fts WHERE title MATCH 'sqlite'; -- Boolean operators (AND is implicit, OR and NOT explicit) SELECT * FROM articles_fts WHERE articles_fts MATCH 'sqlite AND performance NOT slow'; -- Phrase search SELECT * FROM articles_fts WHERE articles_fts MATCH '"write ahead log"'; -- Prefix search SELECT * FROM articles_fts WHERE articles_fts MATCH 'optim*'; -- NEAR proximity search SELECT * FROM articles_fts WHERE articles_fts MATCH 'NEAR(sqlite performance, 5)'; -- Ranked results with BM25 SELECT *, rank FROM articles_fts WHERE articles_fts MATCH 'database' ORDER BY rank; -- Weighted BM25 (title=10x, body=1x) SELECT *, bm25(articles_fts, 10.0, 1.0) as score FROM articles_fts WHERE articles_fts MATCH 'query' ORDER BY score; ``` ### Highlight and Snippet ```sql -- Highlight matching terms SELECT highlight(articles_fts, 0, '', '') as title, highlight(articles_fts, 1, '', '') as body FROM articles_fts WHERE articles_fts MATCH 'sqlite'; -- Extract snippets with context SELECT snippet(articles_fts, 1, '', '', '...', 32) as excerpt FROM articles_fts WHERE articles_fts MATCH 'performance'; ``` ### Sync FTS5 with Source Table ```sql -- Keep FTS5 index in sync using triggers CREATE TRIGGER articles_ai AFTER INSERT ON articles BEGIN INSERT INTO articles_fts(rowid, title, body) VALUES (new.id, new.title, new.body); END; CREATE TRIGGER articles_ad AFTER DELETE ON articles BEGIN INSERT INTO articles_fts(articles_fts, rowid, title, body) VALUES('delete', old.id, old.title, old.body); END; CREATE TRIGGER articles_au AFTER UPDATE ON articles BEGIN INSERT INTO articles_fts(articles_fts, rowid, title, body) VALUES('delete', old.id, old.title, old.body); INSERT INTO articles_fts(rowid, title, body) VALUES (new.id, new.title, new.body); END; ``` ### FTS5 Maintenance ```sql -- Optimize the index (merge segments) INSERT INTO articles_fts(articles_fts) VALUES('optimize'); -- Rebuild from content table INSERT INTO articles_fts(articles_fts) VALUES('rebuild'); -- Integrity check INSERT INTO articles_fts(articles_fts) VALUES('integrity-check'); ``` ### FTS5 Tokenizers | Tokenizer | Description | Use Case | |-----------|-------------|----------| | `unicode61` | Default, Unicode-aware | General text | | `porter` | Stemming (running→run) | English text search | | `trigram` | 3-character subsequences | Substring matching, LIKE replacement | | `ascii` | ASCII-only tokenization | Simple English text | ## JSON Functions SQLite has built-in JSON support. No extension loading needed (compiled in since 3.9.0, always-on since 3.38.0). ### Extract Values ```sql -- Arrow operators (recommended, SQLite 3.38.0+) SELECT data->>'$.name' as name FROM profiles; -- returns SQL value SELECT data->'$.address' as addr FROM profiles; -- returns JSON text -- json_extract function SELECT json_extract(data, '$.name') FROM profiles; SELECT json_extract(data, '$.tags[0]') FROM profiles; SELECT json_extract(data, '$.nested.deep.value') FROM profiles; ``` ### Modify JSON ```sql -- Set a value (insert or replace) UPDATE profiles SET data = json_set(data, '$.verified', json('true')); -- Insert only (skip if exists) UPDATE profiles SET data = json_insert(data, '$.new_field', 'value'); -- Replace only (skip if missing) UPDATE profiles SET data = json_replace(data, '$.name', 'New Name'); -- Remove a key UPDATE profiles SET data = json_remove(data, '$.old_field'); ``` ### Build JSON ```sql -- Create JSON objects SELECT json_object('id', id, 'name', name) FROM users; -- Create JSON arrays SELECT json_array(1, 'two', 3.0, null); -- Aggregate into JSON array SELECT json_group_array(name) FROM users; -- → '["Alice","Bob","Carol"]' -- Aggregate into JSON object SELECT json_group_object(key, value) FROM settings; -- → '{"theme":"dark","lang":"en"}' ``` ### Iterate JSON Arrays ```sql -- Expand JSON array into rows SELECT posts.id, tag.value as tag FROM posts, json_each(posts.tags) as tag; -- Filter by JSON array contents SELECT * FROM posts WHERE EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM json_each(posts.tags) WHERE value = 'sqlite' ); -- Deep traversal with json_tree SELECT fullkey, value, type FROM json_tree('{"a":{"b":[1,2,3]}}'); ``` ### JSON Path Syntax | Path | Meaning | |------|---------| | `$` | Root element | | `$.key` | Object property | | `$.arr[0]` | Array index (0-based) | | `$.arr[#-1]` | Last array element | | `$.arr[#]` | Append position (insert/set only) | | `$.a.b.c` | Nested access | ### JSONB (Binary JSON, SQLite 3.45.0+) ```sql -- Store as JSONB for faster operations INSERT INTO docs (data) VALUES (jsonb('{"key": "value"}')); -- All json_* functions accept JSONB input -- Use jsonb_* variants to return JSONB output UPDATE docs SET data = jsonb_set(data, '$.key', '"new"'); ``` ## R-Tree — Spatial Indexing R-Tree provides efficient spatial queries for bounding-box lookups: ```sql -- Create R-Tree virtual table CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE spatial_idx USING rtree( id, min_x, max_x, min_y, max_y ); -- Insert bounding boxes INSERT INTO spatial_idx VALUES (1, -80.0, -79.6, 35.0, 35.2); -- Query: find all objects overlapping a region SELECT * FROM spatial_idx WHERE min_x <= -79.5 AND max_x >= -80.0 AND min_y <= 35.5 AND max_y >= 35.0; ``` Use cases: geographic queries, range searches, collision detection, nearest-neighbor. ## Math Functions (SQLite 3.35.0+) Built-in since 3.35.0, no extension needed: ```sql SELECT abs(-5), -- 5 ceil(4.2), -- 5 floor(4.8), -- 4 round(3.14159, 2), -- 3.14 sqrt(16), -- 4.0 log(100), -- 2.0 (log base 10) ln(2.718), -- ~1.0 pow(2, 10), -- 1024.0 pi(), -- 3.14159... sin(0), cos(0), -- 0.0, 1.0 random(), -- random 64-bit integer sign(-5); -- -1 ``` ## generate_series (Table-Valued Function) ```sql -- Generate a sequence of numbers SELECT value FROM generate_series(1, 10); -- With step SELECT value FROM generate_series(0, 100, 5); -- Generate dates SELECT date('2026-01-01', '+' || value || ' days') as date FROM generate_series(0, 364); ``` ## Loading Custom Extensions ```sql -- Load from shared library .load ./my_extension -- Or via SQL function SELECT load_extension('./my_extension'); -- With explicit entry point SELECT load_extension('./my_extension', 'sqlite3_my_extension_init'); ``` ### Writing a Custom Extension (C) ```c #include SQLITE_EXTENSION_INIT1 static void my_upper(sqlite3_context *ctx, int argc, sqlite3_value **argv) { const char *input = (const char *)sqlite3_value_text(argv[0]); // ... transform to uppercase ... sqlite3_result_text(ctx, result, -1, SQLITE_TRANSIENT); } int sqlite3_myext_init(sqlite3 *db, char **err, const sqlite3_api_routines *api) { SQLITE_EXTENSION_INIT2(api); sqlite3_create_function(db, "my_upper", 1, SQLITE_UTF8, 0, my_upper, 0, 0); return SQLITE_OK; } ``` Compile: ```bash # Linux gcc -g -fPIC -shared my_extension.c -o my_extension.so # macOS gcc -g -fPIC -dynamiclib my_extension.c -o my_extension.dylib ``` ### Security Note Extension loading is **disabled by default** in the C API. Enable with: ```c sqlite3_db_config(db, SQLITE_DBCONFIG_ENABLE_LOAD_EXTENSION, 1, NULL); ``` The `sqlite3` CLI enables extension loading automatically. ## Common Extensions Ecosystem | Extension | Purpose | Source | |-----------|---------|--------| | `sqlean` | 60+ functions (crypto, text, regex, stats) | github.com/nalgeon/sqlean | | `sqlite-vec` | Vector similarity search | github.com/asg017/sqlite-vec | | `sqlite-vss` | Vector search (FAISS-based) | github.com/asg017/sqlite-vss | | `spatialite` | Full GIS/spatial support | gaia-gis.it/fossil/libspatialite | | `sqlite-http` | HTTP requests from SQL | github.com/asg017/sqlite-http | | `sqlite-regex` | Regular expressions | github.com/nicholasgasior/sqlite-regex | | `cr-sqlite` | CRDTs for sync/replication | github.com/vlcn-io/cr-sqlite |

sections.title: FTS5 full-text search
sections.body: Create FTS5 virtual tables for full-text search. Query with MATCH operator and boolean logic (AND, OR, NOT). Rank results with bm25(). Extract snippets with snippet() and highlight matches with highlight(). Tokenizers: unicode61 (default), porter (stemming), trigram (substring matching). Sync with source tables using triggers. Maintain with optimize and rebuild commands.

sections.title: JSON functions and operators
sections.body: Extract with ->> (SQL value) and -> (JSON text) operators, or json_extract(). Modify with json_set(), json_insert(), json_replace(), json_remove(). Build with json_object(), json_array(), json_group_array(), json_group_object(). Iterate arrays with json_each(), traverse deeply with json_tree(). Validate with json_valid(). JSONB (3.45.0+) stores binary JSON for faster processing. See [[sqlite/queries]] for JSON query patterns.

sections.title: R-Tree spatial indexing
sections.body: R-Tree provides efficient bounding-box queries for spatial data. Create with CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE USING rtree(id, minX, maxX, minY, maxY). Query with range conditions on the coordinate columns. Use cases: geographic lookup, range queries, collision detection, nearest-neighbor searches. Supports 1-5 dimensions.

sections.title: Math functions
sections.body: Built-in since SQLite 3.35.0: abs(), ceil(), floor(), round(), sqrt(), log(), ln(), pow(), pi(), sin(), cos(), tan(), asin(), acos(), atan(), atan2(), sign(), random(). No extension loading needed. Use for calculations, statistics, and geometric operations directly in SQL.

sections.title: Loading and writing custom extensions
sections.body: Load extensions with .load (CLI), load_extension() (SQL), or sqlite3_load_extension() (C API). Extension loading is disabled by default for security — enable via sqlite3_db_config(). Write extensions in C using sqlite3ext.h. Register functions with sqlite3_create_function(), virtual tables with sqlite3_create_module(). Compile to .so (Linux), .dylib (macOS), .dll (Windows). Popular ecosystem: sqlean, sqlite-vec, spatialite, cr-sqlite.
